threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still wait for the bus instead of hopping on a 55. The locals know it’s quicker to walk to the station, especially during the strikes.
Caught a night bus from Brixton last week; the driver was singing along to the radio. Made the 3 a.m. trip feel like a mini concert, weirdly uplifting.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I spotted a street musician playing Bach on a plastic cello near the U-Bahn, I knew Neukölln had woven itself into my life.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg do you find a kebab shop that doubles as an art gallery. Grab a döner while admiring local art—classic Berlin mash-up!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know you’re local when you’ve dodged a startled dog on the tracks at Civic Center, while everyone else just stares at their phones.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy serenading a full Muni bus with his ukulele. Most riders started tapping their feet. Just another day in the Tenderloin.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is just about Gaudí and hip bars, but it’s the local bakeries with fresh ensaïmades that truly make the neighborhood special.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell the newbies—they’re the ones taking photos of the classic pigeons in Plaça Catalunya like they’ve never seen a bird before.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You think a 10-minute wait for a bus is a crisis. Meanwhile, the locals are casually 20 minutes late to everything, no one’s phased.
Last night on the 35, a guy serenaded the whole bus with 90s R&B. By the time we hit Tulse Hill, we were all clapping along, totally forgetting about the strikes.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day the Turkish bakery down the street opened at 5 AM, and the smell of fresh simit woke me up before sunrise. That's when it felt like home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin will you find spontaneous silent discos under the Oberbaum Bridge, where everyone dances while wearing headphones, lost in their own world.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know you’re local when you instinctively duck into a taqueria when the BART's delayed, instead of standing there looking confused.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 21 at Civic Center today, and a guy was rapping his way through the whole ride. Only in SF can a bus feel like a live show.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think 'Gràcia' is just about the parks, but it’s really the casual chats in the plazas that define us. That’s where life happens.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by the way they shy away from ordering tapas without knowing the 'just one' rule—grabbing plates makes them nervous.
